name: Almond Chocolate Creme Brulee
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsweetened Cocoa Powder, firmly packed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, heat cream until bubbles start to form around the edges of the pan. In a large saucepan, whisk together egg yolks, granulated sugar, and cocoa. Slowly add hot cream to yolk mixture, whisking constantly. Place over medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, for 5 to 7 minutes, or until the mixture thickens. The temperature should be approximately 175°F to 180°F on a candy thermometer. Do not boil. Remove from heat and add bittersweet chocolate; stir to blend well.
2. Divide the mixture evenly among 8 ovenproof custard or souffle dishes. Sprinkle almonds over the top, dividing evenly. Cover and refrigerate for 6 hours or overnight. One hour before serving, place all dishes in the freezer.
3. Just before serving, preheat the broiler. Sprinkle the top of each creme brulee with 2 tablespoons brown sugar. Place the dishes under the broiler 5 to 6 inches from the heat and broil for 1½ to 2 minutes, just until the sugar caramelizes. Watch carefully as brown sugar burns easily. Serve immediately. Any leftover creme brulee can be kept in the refrigerator and eaten within a day.
